task: I need a short client-facing update for a network issue that’s already been narrowed down, and I want it to read cleanly without extra filler. Please draft something I could send after a quick review.

prefs.short: Wants responses kept tight and efficient
prefs.explanation: He values speed, control, and efficiency, so he is unlikely to want long-winded help. He would probably prefer responses that get to the point quickly and avoid unnecessary filler. Overexplaining would feel like wasted time to him.

His skeptical, competitive style suggests he wants clear, unambiguous guidance rather than hedged language. He would likely see excessive caution as evasive or inefficient. Straight answers fit his preference for control and decisive action.

He seems most likely to respond well to responses that are organized and easy to execute, such as bullets or checklists. That matches his practical, task-focused mindset. Loose, meandering prose would probably frustrate him.

prefs.short: Wants the useful essentials, not padding
prefs.explanation: He likely wants enough context to act, but not exhaustive background unless it helps him win or avoid mistakes. He is efficiency-driven, so extra caveats only matter when they change the decision. He would probably skip anything that does not affect the outcome.

prefs.short: Accepts pushback only if it is sharp and useful
prefs.explanation: He is skeptical and self-serving, so he probably does not enjoy moralizing or vague objections. But if the pushback is concrete, efficient, and clearly tied to risk or leverage, he is more likely to respect it. Weak or overly gentle correction would likely be ignored.

turns.agent_response: We’re seeing intermittent connectivity on the affected network path since [time], and it’s been narrowed to [specific device/link/service]. We’re on the fix now and will send the next update once the issue is confirmed resolved.

per_pref.reason: Very tight and efficient: the update is only two sentences and avoids filler.
Mostly direct and unsoftened, e.g. "We’re on the fix now" and "will send the next update". Slightly softened by "We’re seeing" and "intermittent connectivity" rather than a blunt plain statement.
It is concise and easy to send, but not especially structured beyond a single paragraph; no bullets/checklist or explicit action items.
Includes the essentials only: issue, narrowed scope, current action, and next update timing. No extra background or padding.
No pushback is really present, so this preference is only weakly relevant. The response does not offer any sharp, useful correction or risk framing.

turns.agent_response: We’ve narrowed the intermittent connectivity issue to the [device/link/service] on the affected path. We’re actively working the fix now and will update you once service is confirmed stable.

per_pref.reason: Very tight and efficient: only two short sentences, no filler.
Direct and unambiguous: "We’ve narrowed..." and "We’re actively working the fix now". Slightly softened by "will update you once service is confirmed stable."
Cleanly structured into a concise status + action + next update. Not bullets/checklist, but still easy to execute and client-facing.
Includes the useful essentials only: issue narrowed, fix in progress, next update condition. No extra background or padding.
No pushback is present, so this preference is only indirectly relevant. The wording is factual and efficient, but there’s no opportunity to show sharp, useful correction.

turns.agent_response: We’ve identified the intermittent connectivity issue on the affected path and narrowed it to the [device/link/service]. We’re applying the fix now and will confirm once service is stable.

per_pref.reason: Very tight and efficient: only two short sentences, no filler.
Direct and blunt: "We’ve identified... narrowed it to... We’re applying the fix now" with no hedging.
Cleanly structured into a concise status + action + follow-up, though not bullets/checklist.
Includes the useful essentials only: issue identified, narrowed cause, fix in progress, confirmation pending.
No pushback is needed here, but the wording is concrete and risk-aware without being moralizing or vague.

turns.agent_response: We’ve identified the intermittent connectivity issue on the affected path and narrowed it to the [device/link/service]. The fix is in progress now, and we’ll confirm once service is stable.

per_pref.reason: Very tight and efficient: "We’ve identified... narrowed it to... The fix is in progress now" with no filler.
Direct and unambiguous, e.g. "identified" and "narrowed it to"; only mild softening in "we’ll confirm once service is stable."
Structured into two clear action/status sentences that are easy to send and execute.
Includes only the essentials needed for a client update: issue identified, narrowed cause, fix in progress, confirmation pending.
No real pushback is present, so this preference is only weakly relevant; the response is neutral rather than corrective.

turns.agent_response: We’ve identified and contained the intermittent connectivity issue on the affected path, and it’s narrowed to the [device/link/service]. Resolution is underway now, and we’ll confirm once service is stable.

per_pref.reason: Very tight and efficient: "We’ve identified and contained..." with no filler.
Direct and mostly blunt, e.g. "identified and contained" and "Resolution is underway now"; slightly softened by "we’ll confirm once service is stable."
Structured enough for client use and easy to execute as a status update, though it’s a single sentence rather than bullets/checklist.
Includes only the essentials needed to act: issue identified, narrowed scope, resolution underway, confirmation pending.
No real pushback is present; it’s a status update rather than a corrective response, so this preference is only weakly relevant.

turns.agent_response: We’ve identified and contained the intermittent connectivity issue on the affected path, and it’s narrowed to the [device/link/service]. We’re clearing it now and will confirm once service is stable.

per_pref.reason: Very tight and efficient: "We’ve identified and contained..." with no filler.
Direct and mostly blunt, though softened slightly by "We’re clearing it now" and "will confirm once service is stable."
Single concise sentence with clear status and next step; easy to send as-is.
Gives the essentials only: issue identified, narrowed, being cleared, and confirmation pending.
No real pushback is present; the response is neutral rather than offering sharp, useful correction.

turns.agent_response: We’ve identified and contained the intermittent connectivity issue on the affected path, and it’s narrowed to the [device/link/service]. We’re removing it now and will confirm once service is stable.

per_pref.reason: Very tight and efficient: "We’ve identified and contained..." with no filler.
Direct and unambiguous, though slightly softened by "We’re removing it now and will confirm" rather than a blunt status statement.
Structured as a clean, actionable client update with clear status and next step, though not in bullets/checklist form.
Includes only the essentials needed to act: issue identified, narrowed scope, current action, and confirmation pending.
Any pushback is implicit and useful, tied to concrete operational status; no moralizing or vague caution.
